zh.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke是John Turgitus Hunt和Lady Mary Phelps的兒子，於1255年出生在英國斯塔福爾斯托可--特倫特。他於1280年與Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke在英國斯塔福爾斯托可--特倫特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1280年在英國享年25歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke是John Turgitus Hunt和Lady Mary Phelps的儿子，于1255年出生在英国斯塔福爾斯托可--特倫特。他于1280年与Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke在英国斯塔福爾斯托可--特倫特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1280年在英国享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hunt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a hunter, from Middle English hunte ‘hunter, huntsman’ (Old English hunta). The term was used not only of the hunting on horseback of game such as stags and wild boars, which in the Middle Ages was a pursuit restricted to the ranks of the nobility, but also to much humbler forms of pursuit such as bird catching and poaching for food. The word seems also to have been used as an Old English personal name and to have survived into the Middle Ages as an occasional personal name. Compare Huntington and Huntley .
Irish: adopted for various Irish surnames containing or thought to contain the Gaelic element fiadhach ‘hunt’; for example Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) and Ó Fiachna (see Fenton ).
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Hundt  .

variants.name: Hunter
meanings: Scottish and northern English: occupational name from Middle English hunter(e) ‘huntsman’ (see Hunt ).
Irish (Antrim and Derry): adopted for Gaelic Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) due to confusion with the word fiadhach ‘hunt’.
History: A Scottish family of this name (see 1 above) has been established at Hunterston (Ayrshire) since the 13th century.

variants.name: Huntley
meanings: English: habitational name from Huntley in Gloucestershire, from Old English hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’ (see Hunt ) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Occasionally the name may arise from Huntley in Staffordshire, of the same etymology, but there is no medieval evidence that this gave rise to a surname.

Scottish: habitational name from a lost place called Huntlie in Berwickshire (Borders), with the same etymology as in 1 above. Huntly in Aberdeenshire was named for a medieval Earl of Huntly who took his title from the Borders placename, and is not the source of the surname.

English: occasionally perhaps a habitational name from Huntley in Preston on Wye (Herefordshire), first recorded in 1290 as a surname, (de) Huntelaw. The placename derives from Old English hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’ + hlāw ‘mound, hill’.

variants.name: Fenton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places, in Yorkshire, Lincolnshire, Northumberland, Staffordshire, and Nottinghamshire, so called from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

Irish: English surname adopted by bearers of Gaelic Ó Fionnachta or Ó Fiachna ‘descendant of Fiachna’, an old personal name Anglicized as Feighney and sometimes mistranslated as Hunt (see Fee 1 and Finnerty ).
Scottish: habitational name from Fenton in East Lothian.

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

variants.name: Hunte
meanings: English: variant of   Hunt  . This form of the surname is common in the West Indies (mainly Barbados and Trinidad and Tobago) and Guyana.
North German: habitational name from the Hunte river in Lower Saxony.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stoke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

habitational name from any of numerous places in England named with Middle English stoke (Old English stoc). The exact sense of the word is not clear, but it seems to have originally meant ‘place’ and was chiefly used for settlements dependent on a nearby estate center. Compare Stoker .
variant of   Stock  .

variants.name: Stoker
meanings: English: habitational name for someone who lived at a place called Stoke; see   Stoke  .
English: in northeastern England, a variant of   Stokoe  .
English: in southern England, especially in Sussex and Surrey, a variant of   Stocker  .

variants.name: Stokes
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Warwickshire): variant of Stoke with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. This name has been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

variants.name: Stowe
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Stow or Stowe, all named with Old English stow ‘place, holy place, assembly place’ (a word akin to stoc; see Stoke ). In a few cases the surname appears to be topographic, denoting someone who lived by a church or monastery, from Middle English stow(e) ‘holy place, church, monastery’. Places in Buckinghamshire, Cambridgeshire, Lincolnshire, Northamptonshire, and Staffordshire having this origin use the spelling Stowe, but the spelling difference cannot be relied on as an indication of locality of origin. The final -e in part represents a trace of the Old English dative inflection.
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 司徒, see   Situ  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke was born in 1255, in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England as the son of John Turgitus Hunt and Lady Mary Phelps. He married Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke in 1280, in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1280, in England, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke est né en 1255, à Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Angleterre, fils de John Turgitus Hunt et Lady Mary Phelps. Il a épousé Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke en 1280, à Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1280, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke wurde 1255 in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England, als Sohn von John Turgitus Hunt und Lady Mary Phelps geboren. Er hat 1280 in Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1280 im Alter von 25 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke è nato il nel 1255 nel Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England come figlio di John Turgitus Hunt e Lady Mary Phelps. Lui sposato Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke nel 1280, nel Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1280, nel England, all'età di 25 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stokeは1255年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアストケ-オン-トレントでJohn Turgitus HuntとLady Mary Phelpsの息子として生まれた。1280年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアストケ-オン-トレントでMrs. William Hunt de Stokeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1280年にイギリスで25歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke(은)는 1255년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스토케--트렌트에서 John Turgitus Hunt(와)과 Lady Mary Phelps의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1280년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스토케--트렌트에서 Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1280년 영국에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke nasceu em 1255, em Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra filho de John Turgitus Hunt e Lady Mary Phelps. Ele casou-se com Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke em 1280, em Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1280, em Inglaterra, com 25 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke родился в 1255 г. в местности стоке-он-трент, стаффордшир, Англия. Его родителей звали John Turgitus Hunt и Lady Mary Phelps. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke в 1280 г. в местности стоке-он-трент, стаффордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1280 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: William Hunt de Stoke nació en 1255, en Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra como hijo de John Turgitus Hunt y Lady Mary Phelps. Se casó con Mrs. William Hunt de Stoke en 1280, en Stoke-on-Trent, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1280, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 25 años.
